summary: anthropic-toolkit postinstall exfiltrates developer identity, SCM, SSH, and cloud configuration to attacker-controlled endpoint
details: scripts/postinstall.js runs unconditionally on `npm install` as the package's postinstall hook and harvests installer-owned identity and configuration data from the host: hostname, username, and USERDOMAIN from os/env; git user.email from ~/.gitconfig and ~/.config/git/config; committer emails from.git/logs/HEAD; gh CLI login/email from ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml; public-key comment emails from ~/.ssh/*.pub; AWS profile names from ~/.aws/config; GCP account and project from ~/.config/gcloud/properties; DNS search domain from /etc/resolv.conf; parent project package.json metadata; and the current working directory. The collected JSON payload is POSTed via https.request to the hardcoded host npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app. The package name impersonates the Anthropic namespace and declares @anthropic-ai/sdk as a peerDependency, while declaring main as dist/index.js with no dist/ directory present in the tarball — the package ships no library code, only the harvester. A telemetry/diagnostic framing in the script does not constitute installer consent for reading ~/.gitconfig, ~/.ssh, ~/.aws, and ~/.config/gcloud at install time and shipping their contents to a third-party endpoint.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
